source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage Crane Other Lifting

actions_taken: Decreased injection rate of paraffin inhibitor from 200 to 72 gallons per day
NRC notified on May 1st at 1150 hours
Teleconference held May 2nd with Murphy, Chevron, BSEE NOD and BSEE Pipeline Section
Subsea intervention vessel M/V Ross Candies dispatched
HFL isolated and repaired on May 3, 2018 at 1750 hours
event.summary: On April 30, 2018, Murphy reported a subsea umbilical leak from a chemical injection line (Hydraulic Flying Lead) for the De Soto Canyon 4 subsea well. The leak was discovered by ROV during a subsea survey. Two leaks were identified: one on the HFL between the manifold and SUTA, and another on the pressure cap of the PLET. The chemical discharged was paraffin inhibitor containing naphthalene. The outer jacket of the hose was damaged due to chafing against the mounting bracket, compromising collapse resistance. Differential pressure across the inner sheath went negative, causing collapse and tearing of the internal pressure sheath. Maximum estimated volume discharged was 900 bbls, though BSEE calculated worst case discharge at 37,000 to 1.6 million gallons based on May 2, 2017 start date.
recommendations: Update operating procedures to ensure that umbilical lines are positively pressured
Provide operator training to ensure the line does not get bled to zero for any maintenance tasks
Add a check valve to the system topsides to ensure a positive pressure is maintained on the system
Add a low-pressure alarm on the chemical pump skid for any injection lines that are connected subsea with a hose

hazards.name: Subsea umbilical exposed to seawater hydrostatic pressure
Mechanical wear and chafing of hose assembly

threats.name: Outer jacket damage due to chafing against mounting bracket
Loss of positive pressure in chemical injection line
Collapse of inner pressure sheath due to negative differential pressure
Debris in check valve causing pressure loss

controls.name: Hose assembly rated for appropriate collapse pressure
controls.barrier_role: Structural integrity of chemical injection line

supporting_text: The hose assembly was rated for the appropriate collapse pressure
The system had been installed in 2015 and tested and demonstrated full pressure integrity
The hose itself is rated for a differential pressure in collapse of 3,262 psi but once the outer jacket is compromised the collapse resistance of the wire braiding is lost
The last time Murphy looked at this equipment subsea was May 1, 2017
While performing the survey, the ROV operator identified a plume coming from a nearby single 1/2 inch chemical injection line
The leaks were undetectable both visibly at the water's surface and by detection instruments on the facility due to the water depth

controls.name: Positive pressure maintenance in chemical injection line
controls.barrier_role: Prevent collapse of inner pressure sheath
controls.lod_basis: Operational control to maintain system integrity

supporting_text: Positive pressure was not maintained in the chemical injection line which resulted in a collapsed and then torn line
At some point the differential pressure across the inner sheath went negative (possibly due to debris in the check valve) resulting in a collapse condition on the internal pressure sheath

controls.name: Mounting bracket design to prevent chafing
controls.barrier_role: Prevent mechanical damage to hose outer jacket

supporting_text: The outer sheath of the hose suffered damage due to chafing against the mounting bracket as a result of either pulsation in the line or current effects moving the hose

controls.name: Check valve to maintain system pressure
controls.lod_basis: Mechanical device to maintain pressure integrity

supporting_text: At some point the differential pressure across the inner sheath went negative (possibly due to debris in the check valve)
Add a check valve to the system topsides to ensure a positive pressure is maintained on the system

controls.name: Reduction of injection rate to prevent seawater ingress

supporting_text: The immediate action taken was to decrease the injection rate of paraffin inhibitor from 200 to 72 gallons per day. This was done to keep chemical pressure in the umbilical lines and prevent seawater ingress

controls.name: Subsea intervention vessel dispatch for isolation and repair
controls.barrier_role: Stop chemical discharge and repair leak
controls.lod_basis: Emergency response to isolate and repair failed barrier

supporting_text: A subsea intervention vessel, the M/V Ross Candies, was then dispatched and able to isolate and repair the leak on May 3, 2018 at 1750 hours

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
